summary: Pre-rechunk DB cleanup: audit + clear Orphic chunk_id references before chunk_id space changes
description:
## Why this exists

C2 (dc8a8e00) changes Orphic Hymns chunking from "one chunk per source page" to "one chunk per Roman-numeral hymn". This is a destructive chunk_id change — old IDs like 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.063' vanish; new IDs use the Roman numeral.

C3 (10917af0) runs chunk.py + embed_corpus.py --reindex, but those only touch corpus/*.toml and chunk_embeddings. They do NOT clean up the rest of the DB tables that key on chunk_id. Without this ticket, the live DB will have hundreds of orphaned rows after rechunk: dangling FKs, broken EXPRESSES edges to nonexistent chunks, staged_edges pointing at deleted source/target chunks, etc.

This ticket runs BEFORE C3 (10917af0) and AFTER C2 (dc8a8e00).

## Tables affected

Every table where Orphic chunk_ids appear:

- **nodes** — chunk node rows (~141 rows). Will be replaced by chunk.py via INSERT OR REPLACE on next pipeline run, BUT only for the new IDs. Old IDs need explicit DELETE.
- **edges** — any row where source_id or target_id is an Orphic chunk_id:
  - EXPRESSES edges (Orphic chunk → concept) from prior tagging
  - PARALLELS / CONTRASTS edges (Orphic chunk ↔ other-tradition chunk), including the 210 [auto]-promoted PARALLELS just merged in PR #7. Many of those involve Orphic chunks per the post-promote audit.
- **staged_tags** — Orphic chunk_id rows from any tag_concepts.py runs.
- **staged_edges** — pending and accepted rows where source_chunk OR target_chunk is Orphic.
- **chunk_embeddings** — covered by --reindex, but stale rows for vanished IDs leak. Need explicit DELETE.

## Proposed approach

1. **Snapshot first.** \`sqlite3 data/guru.db \".backup '~/guru-backups/guru-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)-pre-orphic-rechunk.db'\"\` + integrity_check.

2. **Audit query** — count rows that will be invalidated:
   \`\`\`sql
   SELECT 'nodes',         COUNT(*) FROM nodes WHERE id LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%' UNION ALL
   SELECT 'edges (src)',   COUNT(*) FROM edges WHERE source_id LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%' UNION ALL
   SELECT 'edges (tgt)',   COUNT(*) FROM edges WHERE target_id LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%' UNION ALL
   SELECT 'staged_tags',   COUNT(*) FROM staged_tags WHERE chunk_id LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%' UNION ALL
   SELECT 'staged_edges',  COUNT(*) FROM staged_edges WHERE source_chunk LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%' OR target_chunk LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%' UNION ALL
   SELECT 'embeddings',    COUNT(*) FROM chunk_embeddings WHERE chunk_id LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%';
   \`\`\`

3. **Cleanup SQL** — inside one transaction:
   \`\`\`sql
   BEGIN;
   DELETE FROM edges            WHERE source_id   LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%';
   DELETE FROM edges            WHERE target_id   LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%';
   DELETE FROM staged_tags      WHERE chunk_id    LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%';
   DELETE FROM staged_edges     WHERE source_chunk LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%' OR target_chunk LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%';
   DELETE FROM chunk_embeddings WHERE chunk_id    LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%';
   DELETE FROM nodes            WHERE id          LIKE 'Greek Mystery.orphic-hymns.%';
   COMMIT;
   \`\`\`

4. **Post-clean verification**:
   - integrity_check still ok
   - No rows remaining matching the LIKE pattern in any of the above tables
   - Live edge count for non-Orphic types/tiers unchanged (compare to manifest)

## What this DOES delete (intentionally, with curator awareness)

- Orphic-keyed [auto] PARALLELS / CONTRASTS edges from PR #7's auto-promote run. These will be re-proposed on the next propose_edges.py run after rechunk; the new chunk_ids will be different, so re-running auto-promote with the same confidence floor (0.9) gives equivalent coverage.
- Orphic-keyed staged_edges (both pending and accepted/rejected/reclassified).
- Orphic-keyed staged_tags (any pending/accepted from prior tag_concepts.py runs).

The 3 pre-existing verified PARALLELS edges (Logion 77 ↔ Sefer Yetzirah, Logion 24 ↔ Sefer Yetzirah, Tao Te Ching 48 ↔ Logion 2) do NOT involve Orphic chunks per the PR #7 audit, so they survive intact.

## Deliverable

Either a one-shot script (\`scripts/cleanup_orphic_pre_rechunk.sh\` mirroring auto_promote_edges.sh — snapshot + audit + transaction-wrapped cleanup) OR an inline migration under scripts/migrations/ if we treat this as a schema-level operation. Lean toward the script since it's a one-off, not a structural change.

## Constraint

Pipeline-driven, not hand-edits. The DELETEs are issued by SQL inside a transaction, not by editing TOML files. Per the project memory rule: "regenerate via tools, never hand-edit corpus".

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Implementation of dc8a8e00 turned out to preserve chunk_id space: 141 raw pages → 141 chunks both before and after the chunker fix. Each raw orphic-hymns-NN.txt produces exactly one chunk numbered NN, regardless of section content changes. So the original heavy cleanup (DELETE from nodes/edges/staged_*) is NOT NEEDED. The 210 [auto] PARALLELS edges, all EXPRESSES tags, all staged_edges/staged_tags rows on Orphic chunk_ids stay valid by reference. What IS needed: re-embed, since embeddings were computed against the noisy bodies (with 'Sacred Texts Classics Index' nav cruft) and need to be regenerated against the clean bodies. Downscope to: snapshot + audit + re-embed via embed_corpus.py --reindex. The original 6-table DELETE transaction is not needed for THIS chunker change; the script keeps audit-only behavior so it remains useful for future chunker changes that DO change chunk_id space.

resolution.note: scripts/cleanup_orphic_pre_rechunk.sh — audit always runs; --apply takes snapshot + runs embed_corpus.py --reindex scoped to orphic-hymns; --force-reset-staged additionally resets Orphic staged_edges to pending. Reduced scope vs original spec: chunk_id space turned out to be preserved by dc8a8e00, so heavy DELETE transaction wasn't needed. Re-embed is the actual cleanup.
